data.title: Bets against utility stocks rise in November; Alliant Energy most shorted
data.description:
Bets against Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLU) increased in November compared to the end of October, with Alliant Energy (LNT) being the most shorted stock in the sector and Eversource Energy (ES) taking the spot for the least shorted.

Average short interest across S&P 500 utilities stocks was 3.02% of shares float at the end of November, up from 2.82% at the end of October.

The S&P 500’s Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLU) was nearly 13.6% so far this year, compared to the broader S&P500 market’s gain of over 17% during the same period.

Stocks with the least and largest short positions

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

Alliant Energy (LNT) had 19.44 million shares sold short as of November, or 7.58% of shares float.

CenterPoint Energy (CNP) was the second-most shorted stock with short interest of 5.81%, followed by Evergy (EVRG) with 5.50%, Xcel Energy (XEL) with 5.47%, and Pinnacle West Capital (PNW) with 5.14%.

Eversource Energy (ES) was the least shorted stock, with 4.46 million shares sold short as of November, or 1.19% of shares float.

Public Service Enterprise Group (PEG) was the second-least shorted stock, with short interest of 1.39%, followed by NextEra Energy (NEE) with 1.55%, PG&E (PCG) with 1.63% and DTE Energy (DTE) with 1.87%.

Industry analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

 

 

Water Utilities was the most shorted industry within the sector, with 4.25% short interest as of November end, which increased from 3.47% a month ago.

Gas Utilities stood at the second spot with 3.48% short interest as of the end of November, up from 3.40% at the end of October.

Multi utilities stocks took the third spot with 3.32% short interest as of November end, compared to 3.13% at the end of October.

Independent Power & Renewable Electricity Producers stood at the fourth spot with 2.94% short interest as of the end of November, marginally down from 2.95% at the end of October.

Bets against the Electric Utilities sector as a whole have increased to 2.89% at the end of November, compared to 2.67% a month ago.

data.title: Comcast, Amazon launch Luna cloud gaming on Xfinity’s entertainment devices
data.description:
• Comcast (CMCSA) and Amazon (AMZN) on Thursday announced the launch of Amazon Luna, a cloud gaming service, on Xfinity TV and streaming devices across the U.S.
  • Starting Thursday, Xfinity customers with an eligible X1 or Xfinity Xumo Stream Box can have access to Amazon Luna’s video games such as Hogwarts Legacy and Indiana Jones and the Great Circle, included with Prime at no additional cost, the companies said.
  • To access Amazon Luna on Xfinity, customers can simply say “Luna” into their voice remote and sign in with either an Amazon Prime or Luna Premium subscription to get started playing.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Netflix set to snap six-session losing streak
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) shares set to snap a six-session losing streak, as the stock is 1.7% higher at $94.28 during afternoon trading on Thursday.

The streaming giant lost nearly 11% in the preceding six sessions. NFLX is down over 16% in the past month. The stock closed up 4.14% on Wednesday at $92.71.

The stock has gained nearly 4.5% so far this year, compared to the 17% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, NFLX has a Hold rating with a score of 3.08 out of 5. The company received an A+ for profitability, an A- for growth, but a D- in the valuation factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 28 out of 42 analysts gave NFLX a Buy and above. 13 analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, and one recommended Strong Sell.

Last week, Netflix (NFLX) said it would buy Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) studio and streaming assets in a deal with an enterprise value of $82.7B.

President Donald Trump said on Sunday that Netflix's (NFLX) $72B deal for assets from Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) will need government review, according to Bloomberg.

However, a high-stake battle is unfolding as Paramount Skydance (PSKY) launched a hostile bid for the entire company on Monday.

Seeking Alpha analysts are cautious and see the stock as a Hold.

“Netflix’s proposed $72 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery (or, for Skydance/Paramount $108 billion) faces steep antitrust scrutiny, high debt, and significant integration risks,” Seeking Alpha analyst Harold L. Vogel highlighted, rating the stock as Sell.

Vogel added that Netflix’s debt would surge to nearly $100 billion post-acquisition, increasing its exposure to long-term interest rate movements and financial risk.

data.title: Kalshi announces launch of a coalition for prediction markets
data.description:
Kalshi (KALSHI) and the crypto exchange company Crypto.com announced on Thursday a coalition for prediction markets, which include Coinbase (COIN), Robinhood (HOOD), and the sports gaming platform Underdog.

The organization is dedicated to preserving safe, transparent, and federally supervised access to prediction markets.

The coalition's early work will focus on reinforcing the federal framework governing prediction markets.

"The U.S. is the biggest frontier for prediction markets, and the momentum we’re seeing makes a unified industry voice not just important, but necessary," said Matt David, executive board member of the coalition and president of North America and chief corporate affairs officer at Crypto.com.

"From day one, we wanted to be regulated. We spent years working with the CFTC because prediction markets must operate with strong federal safeguards that prevent insider trading, protect consumers, and ensure these markets remain transparent and corruption-free," said Sara Slane, executive board member of the coalition and head of corporate development at Kalshi.

data.title: Jump in initial jobless claims signals holiday volatility rather than trouble, economists say
data.description:
The number of initial jobless claims in the U.S. rose by the most in five years, but that's largely a result of the volatility around the Thanksgiving holiday, economists said on Thursday.

For the week ended Dec. 6, initial jobless claims increased by 44K, to 236K, the Department of Labor said. That exceeded the consensus estimate of 220K. Meanwhile, continuing jobless claims for the week ended Nov. 29 fell to 1.838M from 1.937M in the prior week, less than the 1.950M consensus.

"Don’t read too much into the jump in jobless claims," said Heather Long, chief economist at Navy Federal Credit Union, saying the Bureau of Labor Statistics apparently had issues adjusting for the Thanksgiving holiday. "There was an unusually large decline in new claims two weeks ago, and now the data is showing a surge. Smoothing it out, this still looks like an economy averaging 215,000 to 220,000 new jobless claims a week."

Joseph Brusuelas, RSM US's chief economist, takes a similar tone: "Don’t be fooled by seasonal head fakes. It’s more noise than signal. The time of year, one gets big seasonal swings in both directions." He advises waiting until after the Presidents' Day holiday and look at the 13-week moving average for a better sense of the pace of firings.

Navy Federal Credit Union's Long said the hiring rate  is more concerning. "The bigger issue in the labor market is the anemic hiring rate of 3.2%, which is one of the lowest levels since the Great Recession. Americans have now experienced almost a full year of frozen hiring, and it’s zapping consumer confidence, leaving young graduates out of any jobs, and creating an economic drag," she said.

Gisela Young, an economist at Citi, notes that "initial claims rebounded this week after falling sharply during Thanksgiving week with the average of the two weeks at 214K, still a low level. Continuing claims also fell sharply during Thanksgiving week and will likely rebound in the report out next week."

"Continuing claims, apart from this week, have been following a somewhat similar pattern as the same period last year, which, in part, has us expecting stronger job growth in November at 80K after a 45K decline in October," Citi's Young said.

Next week, we'll get the BLS's delayed Employment Situation report for November, which will also include some October data. The October jobs report was canceled because data wasn't collected during the government shutdown that lasted from Oct. 1 to Nov. 12.

data.title: SentinelOne remains undervalued despite transition to AI-native security platform: Citron
data.description:
SentinelOne (S) remains undervalued following its transition from an endpoint security vendor to an AI-native data and security platform, according to Citron Research.

SentinelOne shares have declined more than 30% year to date.

Citron set a $32 price target on the stock, highlighting its third-quarter fiscal 2026 financial results and its recent involvement in helping crack the Salt Typhoon hacking case.

Salt Typhoon was a Chinese-government-linked hacker group that made waves when it broke into multiple global telecommunication company networks in September 2024. T-Mobile (TMUS), Verizon (VZ), AT&T (T) Charter Communications (CHTR), Fortinet (FTNT) and Cisco (CSCO) were among those affected. One of the biggest headlines from the event was the targeting of personal phone data on U.S. President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance during the election campaign.

"This week, SentinelLabs broke the most significant attribution story in the Salt Typhoon investigation — the largest telecom breach in U.S. history," said Citron Research in an investor report. "Their researcher traced two Chinese state hackers back to Cisco's own training program. Wired picked it up. National security circles are buzzing."

"The irony? Salt Typhoon exploited vulnerabilities in Cisco, Palo Alto (PANW), and Fortinet (FTNT) devices — the legacy infrastructure incumbents that trade at premium multiples while SentinelOne trades at 5x sales like it's going out of business," Citron added.

Citron also noted that SentinelOne's emerging products, such as cloud, data and AI, accounted for half of its new contract value in its latest earnings results. The firm highlighted its Purple AI platform.

"Purple AI is the industry's most advanced generative AI security analyst, and it represents SentinelOne's clearest competitive moat," Citron said. "Unlike competitors bolting AI features onto legacy architectures, Purple AI was built natively into the Singularity Platform from inception."

A recent IDC study found that Purple AI presents a 338% three-year return on investment, 55% faster threat remediation and a 60% reduced chance of major security events.

"SentinelOne is priced like BlackBerry (BB) but growing like CrowdStrike (CRWD)," Citron added. "That disconnect creates one of the most compelling risk/reward opportunities in software."

data.title: Rivian develops AI chip for autonomous driving, unveils Autonomy+
data.description:
Rivian Automotive (RIVN) revealed a new custom chip and artificial intelligence models to expand its autonomous driving ambitions at the company’s Autonomy and AI Day  in Palo Alto, California.

At the event, the EV manufacturer also said it plans to offer an Autonomy+ subscription providing hands-free driving for an upfront purchase of $2,500 or $49.99 per month, a more competitive offering compared to Tesla’s (TSLA) $99 per month subscription.

New models will come equipped with lidar sensors, Rivian’s new custom chip (which will replace Nvidia and Qualcomm chips), and an “autonomy computer” to allow for Level 4 self-driving capabilities, underscoring the company’s robotaxi ambitions.

SAE Level 4, or high automation driving, is defined as one in which the car can handle all driving tasks within specific conditions without human intervention, though not considered “full self-driving.” 

By comparison, Tesla (TSLA) vehicles are SAE Level 2, requiring driver supervision and readiness to take over.

Rivian (RIVN) shares are down more than 3% but are off their session lows as a result of the recent development.

data.title: Corbus declines following early-stage results on oral obesity asset CRB-913
data.description:
• Corbus (CRBP) is off ~3% in Thursday trading after reporting data from its phase 1 a single ascending dose and multiple ascending dose study of CRB-913, its oral candidate for chronic obesity.
  • The SAD portion included eight cohorts that received ascending doses of CRB-913 up to a maximum of 600 mg/day or placebo. The MAD portion had four cohorts who received ascending doses of 25 mg, 75 mg or 150 mg daily or placebo. Only one of the cohorts in each portion enrolled obese patients
  • The MAD cohort of obese patients achieved a mean placebo-adjusted weight loss of 2.9% by day 14. Efficacy results were not disclosed for the SAD portion.
  • Corbus noted that drug was well tolerated with no serious treatment-emergent adverse events reported in any of the cohorts, nor any reports of nausea, vomiting, or constipation. However, there were three events of mild anxiety and one of mild irritability in the obese MAD cohort at 150 mg/day, though they were short-lived and didn't require medical intervention.

data.title: Charter Communications most shorted S&P 500 communications stocks in November; Alphabet least shorted
data.description:
Bets against communication services stocks rose in November compared to the end of October, with Charter Communications (CHTR) being the most shorted stock in the sector and Google-parent Alphabet (GOOG) taking the spot for the least shorted.

Average short interest across S&P 500 communications services stocks was 2% of shares float at the end of November, up from 1.83% in October.

The S&P 500’s communications services sector (XLC) is up nearly 21% so far this year, compared to the 17% rise in the broader market.

Stocks with the largest and least short positions

Ranked by short interest as a percentage of shares float

 

Meta Platforms Class A (META), Alphabet Class A (GOOGL) and Netflix (NFLX), the three largest contributors to the index, had short interest of 1.49%, 1.37%, and 1.89%, respectively.

Charter Communications (CHTR) had 16.96 million shares short as on Nov-end, or 19.05% of shares float.

TKO Group (TKO) was the second most shorted stock at 11.08% of shares float, followed by Live Nation Entertainment (LYV) and The Trade Desk (TTD), with short interest of 10.75% and 10.60%, respectively.

Alphabet (GOOG) was the least shorted stock, having 41.67 million shares sold.

Industry Analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

 

 

Media stocks were the most shorted industry within the sector, with 3.59% short interest as of end-November, up from 3.58% during end-October.

Wireless Telecommunication Services took the second spot with 3.11% short interest as of November, compared to 2.57% at October-end.

Interactive Media and Services, which has stocks like Alphabet and Meta Platforms, was the least shorted industry in the sector, with 1.22% short interest as of November.

data.title: Analysts expect the Altria CEO transition to be seamless
data.description:
Altria (MO) traded slightly higher in Thursday trading after announcing that CEO Billy Gifford will be retiring and current CFO Salvatore Mancuso will succeed him. The CEO switch will be effective on May 14, 2026. In addition, the board elected Heather Newman as CFO.

Goldman Sachs analyst Bonnie Herzog said she viewed the announcement positively on the expectation for a seamless transition and limited disruption of Altria's (MO) ongoing transformation of its vision to Move Beyond Smoking and its 2028 Enterprise Goals, even if the leadership announcement was a little earlier than expected. "Mancuso's appointment as CEO was a result of MO's long-term succession planning process, including the assessment of multiple internal and external candidates," updated Herzog. She added that during Gifford's tenure at Altria (MO) he demonstrated strong leadership capabilities and a thoughtful and strategic approach to managing the business.

Bank of America analyst Lisa Lewandowski noted that Mancuso and Newman are known to investors, and the firm expects the transition to be smooth. "We would not expect any changes in mgmt’s commitment to shareholder returns," advised Lewandowski.

Shares of Altria (MO) were up 1.0% in early afternoon trading to $59.27 vs. the 52-week range of $50.08 to $68.60. The dividend yield for new buyers of the stock is 7.2%.
